test_cases.description: walk_response_schema should flatten anyOf with union semantics, discovering leaf fields from all branches — not just allOf
test_cases.input_description: Schema with anyOf containing two object branches, each with distinct leaf fields
test_cases.expected_output_description: WalkResult with FieldEntry for every leaf across all anyOf branches
assertions: All leaf fields from both anyOf branches appear in result.fields
No intermediate object nodes in result.fields

test_cases.description: walk_response_schema should flatten oneOf with union semantics, discovering leaf fields from all variant branches
test_cases.input_description: Schema with oneOf containing three variant schemas
test_cases.expected_output_description: WalkResult with FieldEntry for every leaf across all oneOf branches
assertions: All leaf fields from all oneOf branches appear in result.fields

test_cases.description: All discovered field paths must be prefixed with the provided root_path, not a hardcoded default
test_cases.input_description: Flat schema with root_path='custom.prefix'
test_cases.expected_output_description: All FieldEntry paths start with 'custom.prefix.'

test_cases.description: Schema at exactly max_depth should succeed — max_depth is the limit, not off-by-one before it
test_cases.input_description: Schema nested exactly max_depth levels deep with a leaf at the final level, max_depth=3
test_cases.expected_output_description: WalkResult with the deep leaf field discovered successfully
assertions: result.fields contains the leaf at exactly depth 3

test_cases.description: FieldEntry format_hint should capture the schema's format field (e.g. 'date-time', 'email') rather than being empty or hardcoded
test_cases.input_description: Schema with fields having format='date-time' and format='email'
test_cases.expected_output_description: FieldEntry objects carry the correct format_hint values
assertions: At least one FieldEntry has format_hint 'date-time'
At least one FieldEntry has format_hint 'email'

test_cases.description: Schema walker should resolve chains of $ref references (A -> B -> C) discovering leaf fields at the end
test_cases.input_description: Schema with $ref pointing to another schema that itself has a $ref to a third schema with a leaf
test_cases.expected_output_description: WalkResult with FieldEntry from the deeply referenced schema
assertions: result.fields contains the leaf from the final referenced schema

test_cases.description: An empty dict {} schema (not None) should still produce an INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warning
test_cases.input_description: schema={} with valid ref_resolver
test_cases.expected_output_description: WalkResult with at least one INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warning and empty fields
assertions: result.warnings has at least one warning with code INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA

test_cases.description: Schema walker should traverse arrays of objects, producing correct dot-separated paths including the array traversal
test_cases.input_description: Schema with array of objects where each object has leaf string fields
test_cases.expected_output_description: FieldEntry paths reflect the array-object nesting structure
assertions: result.fields contains FieldEntry for leaf fields inside the array items

test_cases.description: Classification should independently assign different tiers to different fields based on which patterns they match
test_cases.input_description: Three fields: one matching RESTRICTED pattern, one matching INTERNAL, one unmatched
test_cases.expected_output_description: ClassificationResult with RESTRICTED, INTERNAL, and PUBLIC respectively; tier_set contains all three
assertions: Three classified fields with three different tiers

test_cases.description: fnmatch patterns with wildcards should match arbitrary subpaths, not just exact strings
test_cases.input_description: Fields like 'response.users.ssn' and 'response.admin.ssn' with fnmatch pattern '*.ssn'
test_cases.expected_output_description: Both fields match the wildcard pattern and receive the configured tier
assertions: Both fields are classified at the pattern's tier, not PUBLIC

test_cases.description: Regex patterns should match against the field path correctly — verify whether partial matches are handled per contract
test_cases.input_description: Field 'response.email_verified' with regex pattern 'email' (partial) vs '^.*\.email$' (exact leaf)
test_cases.expected_output_description: Behavior depends on whether regex uses search or fullmatch semantics — classification should be consistent
assertions: The field is classified consistently with the pattern matching semantics

test_cases.description: When no registry entries are provided, all fields should default to PUBLIC tier
test_cases.input_description: Multiple FieldEntry objects with empty registry_entries list
test_cases.expected_output_description: All fields classified as PUBLIC, tier_set contains only PUBLIC
assertions: All classified fields have tier PUBLIC

test_cases.description: When a field matches three patterns of different tiers, the highest tier (by integer value) must win
test_cases.input_description: Field matching patterns for PUBLIC, INTERNAL, and CONFIDENTIAL simultaneously
test_cases.expected_output_description: Field is classified as CONFIDENTIAL (highest among the three)
assertions: matched_pattern corresponds to the CONFIDENTIAL registry entry

test_cases.description: When assume_worst_on_incomplete is False, an empty schema should NOT inject RESTRICTED tier
test_cases.input_description: Empty schema with assume_worst_on_incomplete=False
test_cases.expected_output_description: Profile does not contain RESTRICTED tier (though it has warnings and schema_complete=False)

test_cases.description: schema_complete must be False when the schema walk produces warnings, even if fields were discovered
test_cases.input_description: Schema with some valid fields but also an unresolvable $ref causing a warning
test_cases.expected_output_description: Profile has both fields and warnings, schema_complete=False

test_cases.description: Empty adapter_slot_id should violate precondition, same as empty node_id
test_cases.input_description: Valid node_id but empty string adapter_slot_id
test_cases.expected_output_description: Error raised for empty adapter_slot_id
assertions: An error is raised when adapter_slot_id is empty

test_cases.description: Empty endpoint should violate precondition
test_cases.input_description: Valid node_id and adapter_slot_id but empty endpoint
test_cases.expected_output_description: Error raised for empty endpoint
assertions: An error is raised when endpoint is empty

test_cases.description: C005 findings should not trigger BLOCK when C005 is not in gate_config.block_on_codes — BLOCK is only if finding code is in block_on_codes
test_cases.input_description: Profile with undeclared tier, but gate_config.block_on_codes=[] (empty list)
test_cases.expected_output_description: SlotDecision with ALLOW verdict despite C005 findings existing

test_cases.description: When INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA is in block_on_codes and profile has INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warnings, verdict should be BLOCK
test_cases.input_description: Profile with INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warnings, all tiers declared, gate_config.block_on_codes=[INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA]
test_cases.expected_output_description: SlotDecision with BLOCK verdict because INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA finding matches block_on_codes

test_cases.description: C005 finding evidence must contain structural_tiers, declared_tiers, undeclared_tiers, and fields_by_undeclared_tier
test_cases.input_description: Profile with CONFIDENTIAL tier not in declared_access.declared_read_tiers
test_cases.expected_output_description: C005 finding has evidence with all required evidence fields populated correctly

test_cases.description: When all structural tiers are declared, there should be zero C005 findings (not just ALLOW verdict)
test_cases.input_description: Profile with tiers={PUBLIC, INTERNAL}, declared_read_tiers={PUBLIC, INTERNAL, CONFIDENTIAL}
test_cases.expected_output_description: SlotDecision with ALLOW and empty findings (no C005 since all tiers are declared)

test_cases.description: When multiple tiers are undeclared, each gets its own C005 finding — not one combined finding
test_cases.input_description: Profile with tiers={PUBLIC, INTERNAL, CONFIDENTIAL, RESTRICTED}, declared_read_tiers={PUBLIC}
test_cases.expected_output_description: Exactly three C005 findings (one for INTERNAL, CONFIDENTIAL, RESTRICTED each)
assertions: Each of INTERNAL, CONFIDENTIAL, RESTRICTED has its own finding

test_cases.description: Each AccessFinding must include the adapter_slot_id from the profile, not just node_id
test_cases.input_description: Profile with specific adapter_slot_id and undeclared tiers
test_cases.expected_output_description: All findings have adapter_slot_id matching profile.adapter_slot_id
assertions: Every finding in result.findings has adapter_slot_id == profile.adapter_slot_id

test_cases.description: A single RESTRICTED tier observed but not declared should produce exactly one FA_A_015 finding
test_cases.input_description: Observed tiers=[RESTRICTED], declared_read_tiers=[PUBLIC, INTERNAL]
test_cases.expected_output_description: Exactly one FA_A_015 finding for RESTRICTED

test_cases.description: FA_A_015 finding messages must include the specific field paths observed at the undeclared tier, not just the tier name
test_cases.input_description: Observed output with specific field paths at undeclared tier
test_cases.expected_output_description: Finding message includes the field paths that were observed at the violating tier
assertions: Finding message contains at least one observed field path string

test_cases.description: FA_A_015 findings must carry the adapter_slot_id from the observed output
test_cases.input_description: ObservedOutput with specific adapter_slot_id
test_cases.expected_output_description: All findings have adapter_slot_id matching observed.adapter_slot_id

test_cases.description: When all observed tiers are declared (including higher tiers), the result must be an empty list, not a list with zero-severity findings
test_cases.input_description: Observed tiers=[PUBLIC, CONFIDENTIAL], declared_read_tiers=[PUBLIC, INTERNAL, CONFIDENTIAL, RESTRICTED]
test_cases.expected_output_description: Empty list returned

test_cases.description: When assume_worst_on_incomplete is explicitly set to False in config_source, the loaded config must respect that value
test_cases.input_description: config_source={'assume_worst_on_incomplete': False}
test_cases.expected_output_description: GateConfig with assume_worst_on_incomplete=False and default block_on_codes

test_cases.description: load_gate_config should accept multiple valid FindingCode values in block_on_codes
test_cases.input_description: config_source={'block_on_codes': ['C005', 'INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA']}
test_cases.expected_output_description: GateConfig with both codes in block_on_codes

test_cases.description: An explicitly empty block_on_codes list should be accepted (no defaults injected when key is present)
test_cases.input_description: config_source={'block_on_codes': []}
test_cases.expected_output_description: GateConfig with empty block_on_codes list

test_cases.description: load_classification_registry should correctly load multiple entries with different pattern types and tiers
test_cases.input_description: Three registry entries: fnmatch/PUBLIC, regex/CONFIDENTIAL, fnmatch/RESTRICTED
test_cases.expected_output_description: Three ClassificationRegistryEntry objects with correct values
assertions: Each entry has correct tier and pattern_type

test_cases.description: The description field from registry source should be preserved in the loaded entry
test_cases.input_description: Registry entry with description='Social security number field'
test_cases.expected_output_description: Loaded entry preserves the description

test_cases.description: DataTier enum members must have specific integer values: PUBLIC=0, INTERNAL=1, CONFIDENTIAL=2, RESTRICTED=3
test_cases.input_description: Access DataTier enum integer values directly
test_cases.expected_output_description: Integer values match the contract specification

test_cases.description: DataTier members must support natural integer comparison consistent with severity ordering
test_cases.input_description: Compare all adjacent DataTier pairs
test_cases.expected_output_description: Each lower tier is less than the next higher tier

test_cases.description: FindingCode enum members should be usable as stable string identifiers matching their names
test_cases.input_description: Access FindingCode string values
test_cases.expected_output_description: String representation matches expected stable identifiers

test_cases.description: FindingSeverity enum must have correct integer ordering: INFO(0) < WARNING(1) < ERROR(2) < CRITICAL(3)
test_cases.input_description: Access FindingSeverity integer values and compare
test_cases.expected_output_description: Ordering matches contract specification

test_cases.description: FieldEntry field_type should reflect the schema's type (e.g. 'string', 'integer', 'boolean'), not a default or empty string
test_cases.input_description: Schema with string, integer, and boolean leaf fields
test_cases.expected_output_description: Each FieldEntry has the correct field_type
assertions: FieldEntry for string field has field_type 'string'
FieldEntry for integer field has field_type 'integer'
FieldEntry for boolean field has field_type 'boolean'

test_cases.description: C005 findings must have code exactly equal to FindingCode.C005, not a string approximation
test_cases.input_description: Profile with undeclared tier
test_cases.expected_output_description: Finding code is the FindingCode.C005 enum member
assertions: finding.code == FindingCode.C005 for each C005 finding

test_cases.description: result.blocking_codes should contain exactly the finding codes that are both present in findings AND in gate_config.block_on_codes
test_cases.input_description: Profile with undeclared tier, gate_config.block_on_codes=[C005]
test_cases.expected_output_description: blocking_codes contains C005

test_cases.description: Each FA_A_015 finding's node_id field must equal the observed output's node_id, not just appear in the message
test_cases.input_description: ObservedOutput with specific node_id and undeclared tier
test_cases.expected_output_description: Each finding.node_id == observed.node_id

test_cases.description: A single field with no registry entries should produce exactly one ClassifiedField at PUBLIC tier
test_cases.input_description: One FieldEntry, empty registry_entries
test_cases.expected_output_description: One ClassifiedField with tier PUBLIC

test_cases.description: The ref_resolver callable must actually be invoked for $ref URIs — not silently skipped
test_cases.input_description: Schema with $ref to a schema containing unique leaf field; ref_resolver tracks if called
test_cases.expected_output_description: ref_resolver was called and the referenced leaf appears in result.fields
assertions: ref_resolver was called at least once
result.fields contains the leaf from the referenced schema

test_cases.description: Profile tiers should be a set/frozenset — no duplicate tiers even when multiple fields share a tier
test_cases.input_description: Schema with multiple fields all classified as INTERNAL
test_cases.expected_output_description: result.tiers contains INTERNAL exactly once

test_cases.description: C005 finding evidence should include the endpoint from the profile
test_cases.input_description: Profile with endpoint='/api/v2/users' and undeclared tier
test_cases.expected_output_description: Finding evidence.endpoint == '/api/v2/users'

test_cases.description: ClassifiedField should record which pattern caused the match, not an empty string or wrong pattern
test_cases.input_description: Field matching a specific fnmatch pattern '*.ssn'
test_cases.expected_output_description: ClassifiedField.matched_pattern == '*.ssn'
